evidence: R2 running-config: Cisco-IOS-XE-ethernet:speed -> value-100; Cisco-IOS-XE-ethernet:duplex -> "half"; Cisco-IOS-XE-ethernet:negotiation -> auto = false
R1 running-config: GigabitEthernet0/0/1 Cisco-IOS-XE-ethernet:negotiation -> auto = true (neighbor is autonegotiating)
R2 operational status: admin_state = up, oper_state = up, speed = 102400000 (~100 Mb), duplex = null, input_errors = 115, output_errors = 0 (input errors present on an up link)
R2 interface operational counters: "input_errors": 115 (shows the symptom on this interface)
R2 interface operational state: "speed": "102400000" (100Mbps), "duplex": null (duplex not negotiated/unknown) — supports mismatch between configured duplex and negotiated state
R1 running-config (GigabitEthernet0/0/1): "Cisco-IOS-XE-ethernet:negotiation": { "auto": true } (far end is autonegotiating)

R2 running-config shows autonegotiation disabled and forced speed/duplex (see above), and R2 interface shows input_errors=115 while oper/up — consistent with a mismatch where the other side negotiated differently

plan_history.intent_description: R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 is experiencing input errors due to a duplex/autonegotiation mismatch (R2 has autonegotiation disabled, speed 100, duplex half; the far end is autonegotiating). Remediate to restore correct link negotiation and stop input errors.
plan_history.problem: Duplex/autonegotiation mismatch on R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 causing input errors.
plan_history.fix_summary: Enable auto-negotiation on R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 to match the neighbor. Risk: link will renegotiate and may experience a brief traffic interruption.

plan_history.intent_description: R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 is experiencing input errors due to a duplex/autonegotiation mismatch (R2 has autonegotiation disabled, speed 100, duplex half; the far end is autonegotiating). Remediate to restore correct link negotiation and stop input errors.
plan_history.problem: R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 is configured with autonegotiation disabled and forced 100Mbps half-duplex, causing duplex mismatch and input_errors against a far-end that is autonegotiating.
plan_history.fix_summary: Enable autonegotiation on R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 and remove forced speed/duplex so both ends negotiate link parameters automatically. Risk: brief link interruption/traffic loss when negotiation changes.

remaining_issues: R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 still has autonegotiation disabled and forced speed/duplex (100Mbps half) — configuration mismatch with far end.
Interface operational counters on R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 show input_errors (115) and duplex is not negotiated (duplex=null), so the duplex mismatch symptom persists.
